source.definition: to run away quickly from danger or a threat to reach safety

explanation.sourceText: The verb "flee" originates from Old English "flēon", meaning to run away or escape.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „flee“ provine din engleza veche „flēon“, care înseamnă a fugi sau a scăpa.

source.additional: An irregular verb commonly used in situations involving danger or escape.

target.definition: a se duce într-un loc sigur pentru a scăpa de o amenințare

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian verb „a fugi“ derives from Latin „fugere“, meaning to flee or escape.
explanation.targetText: Verbul românesc „a fugi“ derivă din latină, de la „fugere“, însemnând a fugi sau a scăpa.

target.additional: Regelmäßiges Verb der 2. Konjugation (-i). Wird oft im Kontext von Flucht vor Gefahr verwendet.

learningTips.text: Remember "flee" sounds like "fleeing" from danger—imagine running fast to escape trouble.
Think of the phrase "flee the scene" in crime stories to recall the meaning of "a fugi".
The Romanian "a fugi" sounds like "a fugitive"—someone who runs away to avoid danger.
Picture a movie hero who must "flee" to survive—this helps link the English and Romanian words.
